cyst_kidney: CT image showing a kidney cyst, characterized by a well-defined, fluid-filled, round or oval lesion.
Kidney cysts appear as hypodense areas with thin, smooth walls and no internal enhancement.
The image may reveal one or more simple cysts, which are typically benign and asymptomatic.
Cystic lesions do not invade surrounding tissue or show solid components.
This class is defined by the presence of non-calcified, non-enhancing fluid collections within the kidney.
kidney_stone: CT image showing a kidney stone, visible as a hyperdense, calcified focus within the renal collecting system.
Kidney stones appear as bright, white spots on CT, often causing obstruction or hydronephrosis.
The image may reveal single or multiple stones of varying size and location.
Kidney stones are solid, mineral deposits and do not enhance with contrast.
This class is characterized by the presence of radiopaque calculi within the kidney.
kidney_tumor: CT image showing a kidney tumor, which may appear as a solid, enhancing mass with irregular borders.
Kidney tumors often distort the normal renal contour and may invade adjacent structures.
The image may reveal heterogeneous enhancement, necrosis, or calcification within the mass.
Kidney tumors can be benign or malignant, requiring further evaluation for diagnosis.
This class is defined by the presence of an abnormal, space-occupying lesion in the kidney.
normal_kidney: CT image of a healthy kidney, showing normal size, shape, and parenchymal structure with no evidence of disease.
Normal kidneys appear as bean-shaped organs with smooth contours and uniform enhancement.
There are no signs of cysts, stones, or tumors in a normal kidney image.
The image serves as a reference for comparison with pathological kidney findings.
Normal kidney CT scans reveal intact renal anatomy and regular tissue density throughout.
